actionUnits.condition: Scene MonoBehaviour_0 (m_Script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) matches Fire.cs with serialized values: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60 (Awake overrides timeToOn to Random[40,60]), onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, minNeighborDownDistance=3, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095. BoxCollider present, no Rigidbody. There are no UnityEvent m_Calls on this object. Goal: cover the automatic ignition path via neighbors. Preconditions needed: at least two neighboring Fire instances must be recognized as neighbors (within 4 units), and at least one of them should be >=3 units lower in y so hasDownNeighbor=true, and those neighbors must notify this instance as on (NotifyNeighborState(true)) such that onNeighbors >= 2. This sets shouldTurnOn=true and timeToOnDelta increases each frame at 2x (downMultiplier) until timeToOn is reached, then isOn becomes true and UpdateFireState(notify:true) plays particle system and notifies neighbors. Wait 25 seconds to allow ignition when downMultiplier applies (effective ~timeToOn/2).

Cover the turning-off path via repeated water hits. After task 1, ensure isOn==true. Repeatedly call Fire.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) such that successive calls are spaced less than maxHitInterval=2s, and continue until cumulative currentHitTime >= totalHitTime=5s. Expected: isTurningOff true during sequence; when currentHitTime >= 5s, fire turns off (isOn=false), shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, currentHitTime=0, UpdateFireState(notify:true) stops particle systems and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: WaterHit requires a Vector3 parameter; a test harness or water system must invoke it since there is no parameterless public method to call directly in this component.

Cover the hit-interval timeout branch that cancels turning-off. With isOn==true, call a single WaterHit(normal) to set isTurningOff=true and start timers. Then wait without any further WaterHit for longer than maxHitInterval=2s. Expected: in Update, when currentHitInterval >= maxHitInterval, isTurningOff resets to false and currentHitTime resets to 0f; the fire remains on. This ensures the branch that aborts the extinguish process is executed.
